# manager.persistence { #spotforecast2_safe.manager.persistence }

`manager.persistence`



## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [save_forecaster](#spotforecast2_safe.manager.persistence.save_forecaster) | Save a single trained forecaster to disk using joblib. |

### save_forecaster { #spotforecast2_safe.manager.persistence.save_forecaster }

```python
manager.persistence.save_forecaster(
    forecaster,
    model_dir,
    target,
    task_name='',
    verbose=False,
)
```

Save a single trained forecaster to disk using joblib.

Public single-model counterpart to :func:`_save_forecasters`.  When
``task_name`` is provided the file is named
``{task_name}_{target}.joblib``; otherwise the standard convention
``forecaster_{target}.joblib`` (identical to :func:`_get_model_filepath`)
is used.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                                                            | Description                                                                                                         | Default    |
|------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| forecaster | [object](`object`)                                              | Trained forecaster object (any joblib-serialisable model).                                                          | _required_ |
| model_dir  | [Union](`typing.Union`)\[[str](`str`), [Path](`pathlib.Path`)\] | Directory to save the model.  Created if it doesn't exist.                                                          | _required_ |
| target     | [str](`str`)                                                    | Target variable name used in the filename.                                                                          | _required_ |
| task_name  | [str](`str`)                                                    | Optional task identifier prepended to the filename (e.g. ``"task_1_lazy"``).  Defaults to ``""`` (standard naming). | `''`       |
| verbose    | [bool](`bool`)                                                  | Print a confirmation message.  Default: ``False``.                                                                  | `False`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                   | Description                       |
|--------|------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Path   | [Path](`pathlib.Path`) | Full filepath of the saved model. |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                 | Description                             |
|--------|----------------------|-----------------------------------------|
|        | [OSError](`OSError`) | If the model cannot be written to disk. |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import tempfile
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.manager.persistence import save_forecaster
>>> model = LinearRegression()
>>> with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
...     path = save_forecaster(model, tmpdir, "power")
...     print(path.name)
forecaster_power.joblib
>>> with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
...     path = save_forecaster(model, tmpdir, "power", task_name="task_1")
...     print(path.name)
task_1_power.joblib
```
